sources.issuing_organisation: American College of Cardiology, American Heart Association, and Heart Rhythm Society
sources.exact_document_title: 2018 Guideline on the Evaluation and Management of Patients With Bradycardia and Cardiac Conduction Delay: Guidelines Made Simple
sources.version: 2018 ACC/AHA/HRS Guidelines Made Simple selection of tables and figures
sources.population: Patients with symptoms suggestive of or consistent with bradycardia or cardiac conduction disease.
sources.clinical_setting: Initial, outpatient, acute, and specialist evaluation of suspected or documented bradycardia and conduction disorders.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for clinician-led history, physical examination, ECG and selected test review, symptom-rhythm correlation, reversible-cause context, and monitor selection. It is old, is a condensed official summary, and does not authorize automatic rhythm diagnosis, pacing, treatment, or referral.

exact_sections.heading: Evaluation of Bradycardia and Conduction Disease Algorithm
exact_sections.locator: Guidelines Made Simple PDF page 9, Figure 1, plus page 10 reversible-cause table
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports comprehensive clinician history and physical examination, ECG, directed blood testing, symptom frequency, selected ambulatory monitoring, and reversible or treatable cause context without generating a diagnosis or test order.

exact_sections.heading: Chronic bradycardia and conduction-disorder algorithms
exact_sections.locator: Guidelines Made Simple PDF pages 11-13 and 23-24
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting symptom-rhythm correlation, symptom frequency, structural-disease context, reviewed ECG or ambulatory monitoring, and observed follow-up when appropriate; pacing and treatment branches are not mapped.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Adults with suspected, diagnosed, or previously treated atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, outpatient, acute, and specialist cardiovascular care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for clinician-led AF detection, risk and cardiac-function assessment, documented care review, and anticoagulation review. Treatment recommendations, named medicines, risk-score conclusions, and doses are not mapped, and UAE pathways require local adaptation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1-1.1.3 - detection, 12-lead ECG, and ambulatory ECG
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1-1.4.3 - stroke and bleeding risk, cardiac function, and personalised care
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led review of stroke and bleeding risk, comorbidities, echocardiography context, symptoms, patient preferences, information, adherence, and a documented care package.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1-1.6.17 and 1.11.1-1.11.2 - anticoagulation discussion and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting clinician-performed benefit-risk discussion, adherence and control review, renal and bleeding context, annual reassessment, and documented decisions without generating a medicine choice or dose.

archive.title: Atrial fibrillation: diagnosis and management
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.population: Adults with suspected, diagnosed, or previously treated atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter.
archive.setting: Primary, outpatient, acute, and specialist cardiovascular care.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis
sources.population: Adults with recent-onset acute or stable chest pain or discomfort suspected to be cardiac in origin.
sources.clinical_setting: Initial, urgent, primary, outpatient, and specialist assessment of recent-onset chest pain.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for clinician-documented chest-pain history, associated symptoms, risk context, examination and reviewed tests, information, and safety-net discussion. It is old and only partial for atypical or established non-acute follow-up; diagnosis, urgency, testing, treatment, and referral decisions are not generated.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1.1-1.1.1.9 - information, uncertainty, test discussion, and recurrent-pain advice
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

exact_sections.heading: Section 1.2 - acute chest-pain history, associated features, examination, ECG, and test context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting pain timing and characteristics, associated symptoms, cardiovascular history and risk, vital signs and examination, and clinician-reviewed ECG or test context; urgent conclusions remain clinician-led.

exact_sections.heading: Section 1.3 - stable chest-pain assessment and investigation context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.
